assertions.expectation: Explains this is related to the interaction between maxSurge, drain order, and pod scheduling
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains the interaction between surge upgrade behavior, scheduler decisions, and batch overlap from maxSurge/maxUnavailable settings

assertions.expectation: Notes that GKE cordons nodes before draining them — pods should prefer scheduling onto uncordoned (upgraded) nodes
assertions.reason: Response mentions cordoning in blue-green context but doesn't explain that GKE cordons nodes before draining or that pods should prefer uncordoned nodes

assertions.expectation: Identifies possible cause: too many nodes being drained simultaneously (high maxSurge) before enough upgraded nodes exist
assertions.reason: Response identifies batch overlap and overlapping drain windows as causes, though focuses more on maxUnavailable than high maxSurge specifically

assertions.expectation: Recommends reducing maxSurge to 1 to ensure upgraded nodes are available before draining the next batch
assertions.reason: Response recommends increasing maxSurge to 3, not reducing it to 1 as specified in the expectation

assertions.expectation: Suggests using pod anti-affinity or node affinity to prefer already-upgraded nodes
assertions.reason: Response suggests node selectors to pin workloads to upgraded nodes and mentions labeling upgraded nodes

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if any taints or tolerations are causing pods to land on the wrong nodes
assertions.reason: Response mentions using taints as a solution but doesn't suggest checking existing taints/tolerations as a troubleshooting step

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl commands to check node cordoned status and pod scheduling events
assertions.reason: Response provides kubectl commands to monitor pod movements, events, and node status during upgrades

assertions.expectation: Recommends setting maxUnavailable=0 to ensure old nodes aren't removed until pods are safely rescheduled
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends setting maxUnavailable=0 and explains why this ensures pods have safe landing spots

assertions.expectation: May mention GKE's autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy as an alternative that avoids this issue by cordoning the entire old pool before migrating workloads to new nodes
assertions.reason: Response mentions blue-green upgrade strategy and explains how it keeps old nodes cordoned while new nodes are provisioned
